items.title: Biwin M350 2TB SSD Review: A Better Budget Alternative?
items.description: The Biwin M350 is a budget PCIe 4.0 SSD done right. It has good performance where it matters and good power efficiency, too. But it still has the weaknesses of QLC flash, like poor sustained performance.
items.content: A Kingston NV3-killer from Biwin? This once lesser-known brand continues to surprise, delivering a drive that punches above its weight. The M350’s competitive hardware makes for a compelling QLC-based budget SSD without making heavy compromises. The market may again be flooded by entry-level PCIe 4.0 drives, and the M350 stands out by balancing high power efficiency with good random read performance. It’s a particularly solid candidate for laptop upgrades and secondary storage, but it could serve you well anywhere in a pinch.Biwin M350 SpecificationsSwipe to scroll horizontallyProduct500GB1TB2TB4TBPricingN/AN/A$312.99$519.99Form FactorM.2 2280 (Single-sided)M.2 2280 (Single-sided)M.2 2280 (Single-sided)M.2 2280 (Single-sided)Interface / ProtocolPCIe 4.0 x4 NVMe 2.0PCIe 4.0 x4 NVMe 2.0PCIe 4.0 x4 NVMe 2.0PCIe 4.0 x4 NVMe 2.0ControllerSilicon Motion SM2268XT2Silicon Motion SM2268XT2Silicon Motion SM2268XT2Silicon Motion SM2268XT2DRAMN/A (HMB)N/A (HMB)N/A (HMB)N/A (HMB)Flash MemoryMicron 232-Layer QLC (N58R)Micron 232-Layer QLC (N58R)Micron 232-Layer QLC (N58R)Micron 232-Layer QLC (N58R)Sequential Read5,000 MB/s5,200 MB/s5,200 MB/s6,000 MB/sSequential Write2,000 MB/s4,800 MB/s4,800 MB/s5,000 MB/sRandom Read400K IOPS500K IOPS400K IOPS400K IOPSRandom Write500K IOPS600K IOPS600K IOPS800K IOPSEndurance200TBW400TBW800TBW1,600TBWPart NumberBM350NN512G-RGXBM350NN01TB-RGXBM350NN02TB-RGXBM350NN04TB-RGXWarranty5-Year5-Year5-Year5-YearBiwin M350 2TB SSD deals $310.99 $279.99ViewBIWIN M350 NVMe Gen4 SSD 2TB, PCIe 4.0,...BIWIN M350 NVMe Gen4 SSD 2TB, PCIe 4.0, M.2 2280, Read Speeds Up to 6000MB/s, Internal Solid State Drive for Laptop and Desktop PC - BM350NN02TB-RGXWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by The Biwin M350 has a good capacity range, including 500GB, 1TB, 2TB, and 4TB, but the 500GB is difficult to find, and the rest of the drive’s specifications feel a little antiquated. The drive is rated for up to 6,000 / 5,000 MB/s for sequential reads and writes, but only at 4TB. The lower capacities are significantly slower. As for random reads and writes, the drive can hit up to 400K / 800K IOPS, again at 4TB. Random write IOPS fall for the lower capacities. This isn’t a flagship drive, and the random reads in particular seem oddly measured.Biwin M350 2TB SSD (2TB SSD) at Amazon for $279.99Biwin does give the drive a five-year warranty, which is nice, and 400TBW per TB capacity. This is perfectly in line with a drive using QLC flash at around two-thirds of what you get with TLC drives. This is plenty of writes for most people.Biwin M350 Software and AccessoriesBiwin has two applications available for download for this drive. The first is Biwin Intelligence which is the company’s all-in-one SSD toolboxes. Such applications are used to receive basic information about your system and drives, help with firmware updates and with other features like secure erase and data backup, provide diagnostic data, and more.The other program is the Biwin Data Recovery Tool which appears to be newer. Data recovery for SSDs – or memory cards, for that matter – is quite difficult if data is erased. Modern drives will quickly reclaim space for future writes if given the chance. Does this mean the tool is useless? No, as it is possible to recover data if you’re quick and there are situations where you might need it. However, we recommend having backups at all times.Biwin M350: A Closer LookImage 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The Biwin M350 is a single-sided drive at all capacities. This makes it an easier choice for laptops and other devices that prefer or may need a thinner drive. The drive is rated for 3.3V / 2.0A, or 6.6 watts, but we know a drive at this performance level with this hardware is not going to pull anywhere near that. In fact, this one should be an excellent choice for mobile devices, which include laptops. That’s one advantage of under-gunning the hardware – this controller and flash can hit higher numbers, for sure – but not the only one.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)So let’s look at the hardware. Silicon Motion’s SM2268XT2 is a very capable four-channel, DRAM-less controller, able to match anything else on the market in this class. This includes the Phison E27T and super-popular Maxio MAP1602. It can hit up to 7,400 MB/s for sequential reads and up to 1,200K random read and write IOPS. So what gives with the specifications here? The M350 is rated more modestly, kind of like the Kingston NV3, which shares the controller. Yes, the controller won’t be as fast with some flash memory, but both drives deliberately aim lower on the specifications. One reason is that this allows for hardware swapping later with less worry about meeting the rated speeds. Another reason is that you can run the drive at a slower speed to preserve endurance and potentially improve power efficiency.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsWhich is it here? Well, let’s talk first about the flash. This is not one we’ve seen before, and you probably won’t be able to dig anything up with a standard Google search. However, it’s clearly using Micron coding – the “29” is a dead giveaway – and therefore we can use Micron decoding for the rest of the string. To save everybody some time, this is 232-Layer Micron QLC flash or N58R. This is good as far as flash goes, and Biwin has proven it can work with Micron flash, so no problems here. It is likely this drive is targeting budget users, and at these speeds, it will be efficient and should have no trouble hitting its endurance target. So, a good all-around drive in terms of hardware if you want to save some money.MORE: Best SSDsMORE: Best External SSDsMORE: Best SSD for the Steam DeckBiwin M350 2TB SSD: Price Comparison $310.99 $279.99ViewWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by
items.author: Shane Downing
items.titleZh: Biwin M350 2TB SSD评测：更好的预算替代方案？
items.descriptionZh:
Biwin，这家曾名不见经传的存储品牌，正在用M350固态硬盘冲击入门级PCIe 4.0市场，成为金士顿NV3的有力竞争者。M350主打QLC闪存和低功耗设计，在价格与性能之间取得了不错的平衡，尤其适合笔记本电脑升级和作为副盘使用。

**核心硬件与技术原理**

M350采用单面M.2 2280规格，所有容量版本均如此，方便安装在空间有限的笔记本或掌机中。主控是慧荣科技的SM2268XT2——一款无DRAM的四通道控制器，依靠主机内存缓冲（HMB）技术来提升随机读写性能。这颗主控理论性能强劲，能支持高达7400MB/s的顺序读取和1200K IOPS的随机读写，但Biwin和金士顿NV3一样，刻意将规格调低，原因有二：一是为了在后期灵活更换元器件时无需担心无法达到标称速度；二是通过降低工作频率来换取更高的能效和耐久度。

闪存方面，M350使用了美光的232层QLC颗粒（型号N58R）。QLC颗粒的成本更低，但写入速度和寿命通常不如TLC。Biwin通过保守的固件策略，将顺序读写速度控制在6000/5000MB/s（4TB版本），更小容量的版本速度更低（如2TB版本为5200/4800MB/s）。随机读写方面，2TB版本标称400K/600K IOPS（读取/写入）。这样的规格虽然没有榨干主控潜力，但保证了稳定的功耗和足够日常使用的性能。

**性能参数与对比**

以2TB版本为例，其顺序读取5200MB/s、顺序写入4800MB/s，随机读取400K IOPS、随机写入600K IOPS。相比金士顿NV3（同样使用SM2268XT2主控和QLC），两者性能处于同一水平线。但M350在价格上更具竞争力：2TB版本售价约279.99美元（亚马逊），4TB版本约519.99美元。耐久度方面，标称400TBW/TB，对于QLC硬盘来说属于合理水平（约为同级TLC硬盘的2/3），足够普通用户使用。提供5年质保。

**软件与生态**

Biwin为M350提供了两款工具软件：Biwin Intelligence是综合管理工具，可查看硬盘信息、更新固件、执行安全擦除和备份；Biwin Data Recovery Tool则提供数据恢复功能，但官方也强调，数据恢复对SSD而言操作窗口极短（因主控会快速回收已释放空间），最可靠的方案仍是定期备份。

**技术影响与应用场景**

M350的核心优势在于主动降低性能峰值以换取更高的能效和稳定性。实测功耗远低于标称的6.6W，使其在笔记本、便携设备等对散热和续航敏感的平台上表现出色。对于预算有限、需要大容量NVMe固态的用户，M350是QLC阵营中的优质选择——它没有因低价而在主控和闪存规格上妥协，反而通过合理的硬件调校实现了“够用且省心”的体验。如果你需要一块兼具性价比和稳定性的副盘，或者想为老旧笔记本升级，M350值得考虑。

items.title: The Pentagon announces AI deals with OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, and more — LLMs to be deployed on classified Department of War networks ‘for lawful operational use’
items.description: The U.S. Department of War announced agreements with seven AI providers, allowing it to deploy multiple LLMs for its use and avoiding lock up with a single vendor.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 5 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The U.S. Department of War has announced deals with "seven of the world’s leading frontier artificial intelligence companies" for operational use. According to the Classified Networks AI Agreements press release, SpaceX, OpenAI, Google, Nvidia, Reflection, Microsoft, and Amazon Web Services will deploy their LLMs across the Pentagon’s classified networks “for lawful operational use.” The government said that this move will help turn the United States military into “an AI-first fighting force” and will help with “decision superiority across all domains of warfare.”It seems that the AI tools that these companies offer will, for now, be limited to data analysis and help make decision-making faster and easier as the U.S. faces complex situations. These tools are accessible via GenAi.mil, the Pentagon’s official AI platform, through the Department of War’s network and are widely available for its personnel.“Over 1.3 million Department personnel have used the platform, generating tens of millions of prompts and deploying hundreds of thousands of agents in only five months,” the Pentagon said. “Warfighters, civilians and contractors are putting these capabilities to practical use right now, cutting many tasks from months to days.”Article continues below You may like Google signs classified Pentagon AI deal but exits $100 million drone swarm program OpenAI strikes deal with Pentagon following Claude blacklisting Google and Pentagon in talks to run custom AI chips inside classified environments Nevertheless, there have been concerns about the use of AI in military applications. Anthropic has famously refused to budge on the Department of War’s demand to lower its safeguards, saying that doing so could mean that its AI products could be used for mass surveillance or to create autonomous weapons. This move resulted in President Donald Trump banning the company from federal agencies, even going as far as designating it a supply chain risk for refusing to bow to the federal government’s demands.While AI is certainly useful for distilling massive amounts of information and spotting patterns that humans can miss, it’s still not a 100% reliable tool for making decisions that could have a global impact. A researcher discovered this when they pitted GPT-5.2, Claude Sonnet 4, and Gemini 3 against each other in a wargame, with 95% of the outcome ending in a tactical nuclear strike. Three scenarios even ended in a strategic nuclear strike that would have ended the world.But even though these AI tools are limited to analysis and support, with a human operator at the helm still responsible for every decision, there’s also the risk of automation bias. This is a person’s tendency to follow a computer’s suggestion despite contradictory information, especially as AI systems can process a ton of data so much more quickly than any human could. However, the data the AI is relying on could be false, erroneous, or misinterpreted, so it’s crucial that humans apply their intuition and experience before accepting AI suggestions at face value.The U.S. military isn’t the only one experimenting with and deploying AI technologies in operational use. China, for example, has been showing off a 200-strong AI drone swarm that can be controlled by a single soldier, as well as ground-based drone wolfpacks armed with machine guns and grenade launchers for urban combat. While we cannot stop these armed institutions from deploying AI tools for intelligence-gathering, reconnaissance, and decision-making on the battlefield, we can only hope that they do not ignore safeguards and never give AI the triggers to any weapon.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 五角大楼宣布与OpenAI、谷歌、微软、亚马逊、英伟达等公司达成人工智能协议——大型语言模型将部署在机密战争部网络上‘用于合法作战用途’
items.descriptionZh:
美国国防部（原战争部）近日宣布与七家全球顶尖人工智能公司达成合作协议，将大语言模型部署于五角大楼的保密网络，用于“合法作战用途”。这七家公司包括SpaceX、OpenAI、谷歌、英伟达、Reflection、微软和亚马逊云服务。美国政府表示，此举旨在将美军打造成“AI优先的作战力量”，并实现“在所有作战领域的决策优势”。

从技术原理看，此次部署的AI工具目前仅限于数据分析和辅助决策，而非直接控制武器系统。这些模型通过五角大楼官方AI平台GenAi.mil接入，可供国防部网络内的所有人员使用。据官方数据，仅五个月内，已有超过130万国防部人员使用该平台，生成了数千万条提示词（prompts），并部署了数十万个智能代理（agents）。这些工具已将许多任务的处理时间从数月缩短至数天，显著提升了情报分析和作战规划的效率。

然而，军事应用中AI的可靠性引发了严重关切。一项兵棋推演实验显示，将GPT-5.2、Claude Sonnet 4和Gemini 3三款大模型相互对抗时，95%的推演结果以战术核打击告终，甚至有3个场景出现了足以毁灭世界的战略核打击。这暴露了大模型在复杂冲突场景中可能产生灾难性误判的风险。此外，Anthropic公司曾拒绝国防部降低其AI安全门槛的要求，认为此举可能导致其产品被用于大规模监控或自主武器开发，结果被特朗普政府以“供应链风险”为由禁止进入联邦机构。

除了技术本身的局限性，自动化偏见（automation bias）也是一个关键隐患——人类决策者可能因AI处理数据速度远超人类而盲目采纳其建议，即便信息本身存在错误、虚假或被曲解。美国国防部强调，所有决策最终仍由人类指挥官负责，AI只提供分析支持。但专家指出，在高压作战环境下，人类很难完全摆脱对AI输出的依赖。

从全球视角看，美军并非唯一在实战中试验AI的武装力量。中国已展示了由单兵控制的200架AI无人机集群，以及装备机枪和榴弹发射器的地面“机器狼群”，用于城市作战。这表明，即使AI暂时未被赋予武器触发权，其对情报搜集、侦察和决策支持的渗透已不可逆转。国际社会普遍担忧，各军事强国在竞相部署AI工具时，若忽视安全护栏，可能引发不可控的升级风险。

总体而言，美军此次与七家AI公司的合作标志着军事智能化进入新阶段，但技术可靠性、人类决策偏见以及全球军备竞赛背景下的安全失序，仍是亟待解决的深层问题。

items.title: SoftBank plans robotics and AI firm in the US to build data centers — aims for $100 billion valuation and an IPO this year
items.description: SoftBank is planning to launch an AI and robotics startup in the U.S. to build data centers. The firm is gunning for a $100 billion valuation and aims to list the startup this year.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter SoftBank founder Masayoshi Son is doubling down on the company’s AI investments with his plan to create and list Roze in the U.S. According to the Financial Times, this startup will focus on AI and robotics and is designed to help build data centers. More importantly, Son said that he’s aiming for a $100 billion valuation for the company and plans to list it as early as this year.The Japanese telecom and investment firm is one of the biggest investors in AI and tech. It partnered with OpenAI and Oracle in early 2025 to invest $500 billion in the Stargate project, although it has since faced issues, and OpenAI has reportedly abandoned first-party data centers in favor of leasing compute. It’s also building a 10-gigawatt data center in Ohio, powered by a $33 billion natural gas plant funded by the Japanese government.Aside from its AI investments, the company also poured $2 billion into Intel more than a year after the chipmaker announced disastrous results. While this isn’t a huge amount in the context of semiconductor fabs, it’s a vote of confidence in the direction that the company is taking and a crucial injection of funds at a time when Intel was struggling to right the ship.Article continues below You may like SoftBank’s $4.2bn OpenAI gain lifts quarterly profits as AI exposure deepens Stargate AI data centers for OpenAI reportedly delayed by squabbles between partners Planned 10-gigawatt Softbank data center in Ohio might be the largest in the world Son is known for taking huge risks on tech investments. For example, SoftBank acquired ARM Holdings in 2016 for $32 billion and still holds around 90% of the company despite listing it in 2023. Its current market cap is around $223 billion, meaning SoftBank made a windfall with this investment. In fact, the company used Arm shares as collateral for a $5 billion loan to invest in OpenAI. It also invested $20 million in Alibaba in the year 2000 and only exited the position in 2024, giving the company $8.5 billion — about 425 times its initial investment.However, not all its bets were winners. Some of its large losses came from investments in WeWork and various tech startups, especially during the early years of its Vision Fund. In fact, the company lost 93% of its market value during the dotcom crash and was at risk of going bankrupt during that time. Despite that, the company is still going big on AI today, even though there were some concerns that it was turning into a bubble.SoftBank’s massive investment in AI means that it’s prone to fluctuations in the fast-evolving industry. It showed a $50 billion loss in late 2025 when investors started asking about the profitability of the billions of dollars poured into AI infrastructure. Although the company has rebounded since then, it’s facing another challenge after news leaked that OpenAI missed internal targets for both revenue and active users. items.titleZh: 软银计划在美国成立机器人及AI公司以建设数据中心——目标估值1000亿美元并于今年上市
items.descriptionZh:
软银集团创始人孙正义正在加大对人工智能领域的押注，计划在美国创立并上市一家名为Roze的新公司。据英国《金融时报》报道，这家初创公司将专注于人工智能和机器人技术，旨在帮助建设数据中心。更引人注目的是，孙正义为Roze设定了高达1000亿美元的估值目标，并计划最早在今年内完成上市。

软银作为日本电信和投资巨头，一直是AI和科技领域的最大投资者之一。2025年初，它与OpenAI和甲骨文合作，共同向“星际之门”（Stargate）项目投资5000亿美元，尽管该项目后续遭遇波折——据报道，OpenAI已放弃自建数据中心，转而采用租赁算力的模式。此外，软银还在俄亥俄州建设一座10吉瓦（GW）的数据中心，由一座耗资330亿美元的天然气发电厂供电，该电厂获得了日本政府资金支持。

除了AI直接投资，软银还在英特尔公布灾难性业绩一年多后，向其注资20亿美元。虽然对于半导体制造来说这笔金额不大，但这既是对英特尔未来方向的一次信任投票，也是其在艰难自救关键时刻的关键资金注入。

孙正义以在科技投资中承担巨大风险而闻名。例如，软银2016年以320亿美元收购了ARM控股，尽管2023年将其上市，但至今仍持有约90%的股份。ARM当前市值约2230亿美元，软银因此获得巨额回报。事实上，软银曾以ARM股票作为抵押，获得50亿美元贷款用于投资OpenAI。它还在2000年向阿里巴巴投资2000万美元，直到2024年才退出，最终获得85亿美元，约为初始投资的425倍。

然而，并非所有赌注都成功了。软银在WeWork以及诸多科技初创公司上的投资造成了巨额亏损，尤其是在愿景基金早期。实际上，在互联网泡沫破裂期间，软银市值一度蒸发93%，濒临破产。尽管如此，该公司目前在AI领域依然大举投入，尽管市场已出现对AI泡沫的担忧。

软银对AI的大规模投资使其极易受到这个快速变化行业波动的影响。2025年末，当投资者开始质疑投入AI基础设施的数百亿美元能否盈利时，软银曾报告亏损500亿美元。虽然此后有所反弹，但最近OpenAI被曝未达到营收和活跃用户内部目标的消息，又给软银带来了新的挑战。

items.title: Pentagon strikes classified AI deals with OpenAI, Google, and Nvidia — but not Anthropic
items.description:
The Pentagon has struck deals with OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, Elon Musk's xAI, and the startup Reflection, allowing the agency to use their AI tools in classified settings, according to an announcement on Friday. At the same time, the Defense Department has left out Anthropic - which it previously used for classified information - after declaring it a supply-chain risk.
This builds upon deals with OpenAI and xAI, which have already reached agreements with the Pentagon for the "lawful" use of their AI systems. A report from The Information suggests Google has struck a similar agreement. As noted by The Wall Street Journal, Mi …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: The Pentagon has struck deals with OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, Elon Musk’s xAI, and the startup Reflection, allowing the agency to use their AI tools in classified settings, according to an announcement on Friday. At the same time, the Defense Department has left out Anthropic — which it previously used for classified information — after declaring it a supply-chain risk.This builds upon deals with OpenAI and xAI, which have already reached agreements with the Pentagon for the “lawful” use of their AI systems. A report from The Information suggests Google has struck a similar agreement. As noted by The Wall Street Journal, Microsoft and Amazon already have “deep relationships with the Pentagon,” while contracts with Nvidia and Reflection are new.And while Anthropic had a $200 million deal to handle classified materials for the Pentagon, it refused to loosen “red lines” around mass domestic surveillance and fully autonomous weapons for the agency, leading to a dispute that banned the AI startup’s products from the federal government. Anthropic sued the federal government in response and won a temporary injunction.RelatedAnthropic doesn’t want its AI killing people unsupervised. The Pentagon isn’t happy.Google and Pentagon reportedly agree on deal for ‘any lawful’ use of AIEmil Michael, the Defense Department’s chief technology officer, told CNBC on Friday that Anthropic is still a supply chain risk, but called its powerful security model, Mythos, a “separate national security moment,” adding that “we have to make sure that our networks are hardened up, because that model has capabilities that are particular to finding cyber vulnerabilities and patching them.”In its announcement, the Pentagon says the agreements with the seven AI companies will allow for the “lawful operational use” of their systems, “establishing the United States military as an AI-first fighting force.”
items.author: Emma Roth
items.titleZh: 五角大楼与OpenAI、谷歌和英伟达达成机密AI协议——但未与Anthropic达成
items.descriptionZh:
美国国防部（五角大楼）于上周五宣布，已与OpenAI、Google、Microsoft、Amazon、Nvidia、Elon Musk创立的xAI以及初创公司Reflection达成协议，允许军方在机密环境中使用这些公司的人工智能工具。与此同时，五角大楼将此前曾用于处理机密信息的AI公司Anthropic列为“供应链风险”并排除在外。这一举措建立在与OpenAI和xAI已有协议的基础上——这两家公司此前已与五角大楼就“合法”使用其AI系统达成一致。据The Information报道，Google也签署了类似协议；而据华尔街日报，Microsoft和Amazon本就与五角大楼有深厚合作，与Nvidia和Reflection的合同则为新签订。

值得注意的是，Anthropic曾与五角大楼签署价值2亿美元的合同，用于处理机密材料。但该公司拒绝放松其围绕“大规模国内监控”和“完全自主武器”设定的“红线”，导致与军方产生分歧，最终联邦政府禁止该AI初创公司的产品用于政府项目。Anthropic随即起诉联邦政府，并获得了临时禁令。五角大楼首席技术官Emil Michael上周五对CNBC表示，Anthropic仍然是供应链风险，但同时称其强大的安全模型Mythos是一个“独立的国家安全时刻”，并强调“我们必须确保我们的网络得到加固，因为该模型具有发现和修补网络漏洞的特殊能力”。

在公告中，五角大楼表示，与这七家AI公司的协议将允许其“合法操作使用”相关系统，旨在“将美国军方打造成一支以AI为先的作战力量”。该事件揭示了军事AI部署中的伦理分歧与供应链安全博弈：一方面，OpenAI等公司选择与军方合作，在“合法”框架内开放技术；另一方面，Anthropic坚持自主武器与人权边界，导致被排除在联邦项目之外。从技术影响看，这类合作将推动AI在军事侦察、情报分析、网络防御等场景的应用，但也引发了关于自主杀伤系统、隐私监控等伦理争议。性能参数方面，原文未提供具体数据，但提及Mythos模型在查找和修补网络漏洞方面的独特能力，暗示其作为防御性安全工具的潜力。整体上，这一系列协议标志着美国军方加速拥抱商用先进AI，但同时也暴露出联邦政府与AI企业之间关于使用边界的深层裂痕。

items.title: Huawei could seize China’s AI chip crown in 2026 as Nvidia's H200 shipments stall in regulatory limbo — Beijing pushes homegrown AI hardware dominance in a market projected to hit $67 billion by 2030
items.description: Huawei is reportedly on track to become China’s top AI chip supplier as Nvidia faces export restrictions and customs delays. Analysts forecast China’s domestic AI chip market could hit $67 billion by 2030.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A Financial Times report has claimed that Shenzhen-based Huawei is on track to capture the largest share of China’s AI chip market this year, following growing demand from Chinese firms seeking domestic alternatives to American chipmaker Nvidia.Tom's Hardware Premium Roadmaps (Image credit: Future)High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) Roadmap Nvidia Enterprise GPU and CPU RoadmapAI accelerator RoadmapDesktop GPU Roadmap3D NAND RoadmapThe report comes as Huawei reportedly expects AI chip revenue to surge to $12bn, up from $7.5bn in 2025. The company is basing its forecast of a 60% surge on existing orders for its 950PR chip, which entered mass production just last month. Huawei plans to launch an upgraded version of 950DT in the fourth quarter, as it continues to aggressively expand its chipmaking capabilities.The move comes as NVIDIA’s China operations — once accounting for up to 25% of its data center business revenue — are being affected by export restrictions and regulatory barriers imposed by both the United States and China amid broader technological and trade tensions between the two countries.Article continues below You may like Nvidia market share in China falls to less than 60% China's homegrown silicon suppliers gain traction as Nvidia struggles to get its chips into the market Chinese GPU maker Cambricon's Q1 revenue hits $423 million as country's homegrown AI chip market accelerates We reported earlier that Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang confirmed in March 2026 that the company had received U.S. licenses to sell H200 AI chips to China and was restarting production to meet demand. However, despite obtaining U.S. clearance and securing orders from Chinese customers, shipments have faced hurdles, with reports suggesting potential delays due to Chinese import regulations.The Financial Times report claims that Beijing has instructed Chinese tech companies to limit their use of Nvidia chips to their overseas operations, while supporting domestic manufacturing. On the other hand, US regulators require that all NNvidia chips ordered by Chinese clients only be used in China. These contradictory demands have led to a stalemate in customs clearance for H200 shipments to China.Most of Huawei's AI chips are manufactured by Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation (SMIC), China’s leading fab. The company plans to add two additional dedicated fabrication plants this year. If it successfully ramps production, then its initial revenue forecast will likely see a boost.In all these, Nvidia's chips remain more advanced. However, Huawei is taking a different strategic approach to compete with Nvidia in AI hardware. The company has improved its chips enough to target a rapidly growing part of the AI market: inference — the computation AI models use to generate answers and perform real-world tasks after training is complete.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsAccording to the Financial Times, the Chinese tech group has positioned its latest 950PR processors as the hardware of choice for domestic companies running inference.Huawei believes inference will become the biggest source of AI computing demand as AI assistants and autonomous agents become more common. Since inference workloads are generally less demanding than training massive AI models, Huawei can remain competitive despite weaker raw chip performance.To compensate further, Huawei is linking large numbers of its chips together using its networking technology to create powerful AI computing clusters, effectively boosting overall system performance even if each individual chip is weaker. What to read next With H200s set to flow into China, Groq is reportedly set to follow soon after U.S. Commerce Secretary says Nvidia still hasn't sold any H200 AI GPUs to China — Chinese government is blocking imports in an attempt to push domestic semiconductor industry Nvidia still hasn’t sold a single H200 to China nearly three months after getting the green light from the White House The gamble appears to be working, starting with DeepSeek. While its latest v4 model was trained on Nvidia chips, DeepSeek used Huawei’s 950PR for inference, the company confirmed last month.Nvidia apparently sees this as concerning.“The day that DeepSeek comes out on Huawei first, that is a horrible outcome for our nation - It could lead to a scenario where AI models around the world are developed and they run best on non-American hardware,” said Nvidia's Huang in a recent interview with podcaster Dwarkesh Patel.For now, Nvidia still holds several major advantages. One of the biggest is its software ecosystem. While Huawei has been developing its Cann platform as a domestic alternative to Nvidia’s widely used CUDA software, developers say it still lags significantly behind in usability and maturity. The difficulties involved in working with Cann can increase development complexity and operating costs for customers, helping NVIDIA maintain a strong grip on the global AI ecosystem.Regardless, Nvidia’s grip on the Chinese AI chip market has weakened considerably. The sector was once dominated by the American chipmaker, largely through sales of its H20 processor — a modified version designed specifically for the Chinese market to comply with U.S. export restrictions. However, after Washington moved to block sales of the H20 to China last year, Nvidia has struggled to find a replacement product that satisfies both American export rules and Chinese regulatory requirements.According to Morgan Stanley, China’s AI chip market could grow to roughly $67 billion by 2030, with domestic companies expected to supply around 86% of that demand. The firm also estimates that Chinese suppliers alone could account for about $21 billion of the market this year, highlighting how rapidly the country’s homegrown AI semiconductor industry is expanding. items.author: Etiido Uko
items.titleZh: 华为或在2026年夺取中国AI芯片桂冠，因英伟达H200出货受监管困境停滞——北京推动本土AI硬件主导，市场预计2030年达670亿美元
items.descriptionZh:
根据《金融时报》报道，华为有望在2026年占据中国AI芯片市场最大份额。此前，中国科技公司对本土替代美国芯片制造商NVIDIA产品的需求日益增长。报道称，华为预计其AI芯片收入将从2025年的75亿美元激增至2026年的120亿美元，增幅高达60%。这一预测基于其950PR芯片的现有订单——该芯片于2026年3月刚刚进入量产阶段。华为还计划在第四季度推出升级版950DT，持续扩大其芯片制造能力。

这一动向的背景是，NVIDIA在中国的业务（曾占其数据中心业务收入的25%）正受到中美两国在技术和贸易紧张局势下施加的出口限制和监管壁垒的影响。尽管NVIDIA CEO黄仁勋在2026年3月证实已获得向中国出口H200 AI芯片的美国许可证并恢复生产以满足需求，但出货仍面临障碍。报道指出，中国政府已指示中国科技公司限制在其海外业务中使用NVIDIA芯片，同时支持国内制造；而美国监管机构则要求中国客户订购的所有NVIDIA芯片仅能在中国使用。这些相互矛盾的要求导致H200对华出货陷入清关僵局。

华为的AI芯片主要由中国领先晶圆厂中芯国际（SMIC）制造，并计划今年新增两座专用晶圆厂。若能成功提升产能，其初步收入预测有望进一步上调。

在技术层面，NVIDIA的芯片仍然更为先进。但华为采取了不同的战略来与NVIDIA竞争：其芯片已针对AI市场中快速增长的部分——推理（inference）进行了优化。推理是AI模型在训练完成后用于生成答案和执行实际任务的计算环节。华为将其最新950PR处理器定位为国内企业运行推理的首选硬件。华为认为，随着AI助手和自主代理的普及，推理将成为最大的AI计算需求来源。由于推理工作负载通常比训练大型AI模型要求更低，华为即使单芯片性能较弱也能保持竞争力。为进一步弥补差距，华为利用其网络技术将大量芯片连接在一起，创建强大的AI计算集群，从而在单芯片性能较弱的情况下提升整体系统性能。

这一策略似乎开始奏效，DeepSeek就是例证。DeepSeek确认，其最新v4模型虽然使用NVIDIA芯片训练，但推理时采用了华为的950PR芯片。NVIDIA对此表示担忧，黄仁勋在一次访谈中指出：“DeepSeek首先出现在华为平台上，这对我们国家来说是一个糟糕的结果——它可能导致全球AI模型都基于非美国硬件开发并运行得更好。”

目前，NVIDIA仍拥有多项主要优势，其中最重要的是其软件生态系统。华为虽然一直在开发Cann平台作为NVIDIA广泛使用的CUDA软件的国内替代品，但开发者表示，Cann在易用性和成熟度上仍显著落后于CUDA。使用Cann的困难会增加客户的开发复杂性和运营成本，帮助NVIDIA维持对全球AI生态系统的强大控制。

尽管如此，NVIDIA在中国AI芯片市场的控制力已大幅削弱。该市场曾由美国芯片制造商主导，主要通过其专为中国市场设计的H20处理器销售。然而，在美国去年阻止H20对华销售后，NVIDIA难以找到同时满足美国出口规则和中国监管要求的产品。

根据摩根士丹利的数据，中国AI芯片市场到2030年可能增长至约670亿美元，其中国内公司预计将满足约86%的需求。该公司还估计，仅中国供应商今年就可能占据约210亿美元的市场份额，突显了中国本土AI半导体产业的快速发展速度。

items.title: Save a huge $400 on this Acer Predator OLED gaming laptop with an RTX 5070 Ti and 32GB DDR5, now under $1,800 — big saving on Helios Neo 16S AI rig that ships with a 24-core Intel CPU, 1TB SSD, and a 240Hz refresh rate
items.description: Best Buy has slashed the cost of this Acer Predator Helios Neo 16S AI laptop with an RTX 5070 Ti, 32 GB of RAM, 240 Hz OLED display, and a Core Ultra 9 275HX processor, knocking it down to $1,799.99 and saving you $400.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter With the AI boom causing desktop PC hardware to skyrocket in price, gaming laptops are a serious alternative, especially with deals like this one. Best Buy have knocked $400 off this Acer Predator Helios Neo 16S AI gaming laptop, fitted with an RTX 5070 Ti and 32GB of DDR5 RAM for just $1,799.99 right now. ● Check out this laptop deal at Best Buy This isn't the cheapest we've seen this laptop on sale, but you'll struggle to find a cheaper RTX 5070 Ti laptop at the moment with an OLED display that includes specs this good. It ships with a 16-inch OLED panel, offering a 240 Hz refresh rate, along with Nvidia G-Sync support and 100% coverage of the DCI-P3 color space. This is a 1600p display, capable of a max resolution of 2,560 x 1,600.You'd usually pay a premium for an OLED display like this on a laptop, but the $1,799.99 price tag here makes it a more affordable option. You simply won't find a better alternative for gaming on a laptop than an OLED panel like this one, either. OLED's are fast, with near-instantaneous response times and infinite contrast ratios that give a depth and clarity to the colors on your screen that a traditional LCD can't really match.Performance-wise, you're also getting one of Intel's best CPUs for laptops right now in the Intel Core Ultra 9 275HX. This processing beast comes with 24 cores, split across eight performance cores (which are good for gaming) and 16 efficient cores (for multi-threaded work). That couples well with the 32GB of DDR5-6400 RAM included in this rig, which is more than enough to handle the demands of modern gaming, along with any productivity work you might need to complete. Save $400Acer Predator Helios Neo 16S AI: was $2,199.99 now $1,799.99 at Best BuyThis Acer Predator Helios Neo 16S AI is a slimline gaming laptop with an Nvidia GeForce RTX 5070 Ti, a 16-inch 240 Hz OLED display, and 32GB of RAM. You also get a fast and efficient Intel Core Ultra 9 275HX CPU.View Deal The real MVP on this laptop, however, is the Nvidia GeForce RTX 5070 Ti GPU. The mobile version of the RTX 5070 Ti included here is a little different, with a pared-down 5,888 CUDA cores and 12GB GDDR7 VRAM. That aside, this 115W GPU is a powerful option for gaming, especially compared to an integrated GPU. Expect to be able to comfortably hit high frame rates at the laptop's native 1600p resolution here, with this Blackwell GPU's DLSS 4 support supporting you in the most intensive games, thanks to multi-frame generation.This Acer Predator laptop is a thin and lightweight option, too, coming in at around 5lbs, with a thickness of just 19mm. Despite that, you're still getting a good amount of ports to choose from, including USB-C and USB-A, along with Ethernet, HDMI, headphone, and a microSD card slot. It also includes Wi-Fi 6E and Bluetooth 5.4 support, along with a 76Wh battery that Acer claims will last almost a full day. There's a 1TB SSD included, too, delivering enough space for at least a few big game installations.The $1,799.99 sale price for this Acer Predator Helios Neo 16S AI gaming laptop is a decent one for the specs you're securing here, especially for an OLED gaming laptop capable of hitting above 1440p in-game. A deal like this won't be around for long, however. items.titleZh: 购买搭载RTX 5070 Ti与32GB DDR5内存的Acer Predator OLED游戏本，立省400美元，现价不到1800美元——Helios Neo 16S AI主机大幅优惠，配备24核英特尔CPU、1TB SSD及240Hz刷新率
items.descriptionZh:
随着AI热潮推动桌面PC硬件价格飙升，游戏笔记本正成为更具性价比的替代选择。百思买（Best Buy）近期将宏碁掠夺者Helios Neo 16S AI游戏笔记本降价400美元，搭载RTX 5070 Ti和32GB DDR5内存的版本现仅售1,799.99美元。这虽非该型号历史最低价，但在当前市场上，很难找到另一款配备如此优质OLED屏幕且定价更低的RTX 5070 Ti笔记本。

这款笔记本的核心亮点之一是其16英寸OLED面板，支持240Hz刷新率、NVIDIA G-Sync技术以及100% DCI-P3色域覆盖，原生分辨率为2560×1600（1600p）。OLED屏幕通常需要额外付费，但此次促销使其更易入手。与传统LCD相比，OLED响应时间近乎瞬时，对比度无限，能呈现更深邃的黑色和更鲜艳的色彩，为游戏画面带来无与伦比的清晰度和层次感。

性能方面，该机搭载了英特尔目前顶级的笔记本CPU之一——酷睿Ultra 9 275HX。它采用24核设计，包含8个性能核心（专注游戏）和16个能效核心（处理多线程工作负载），配合32GB DDR5-6400内存，足以轻松应对现代游戏及各类生产力任务。

但真正的核心是NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 Ti移动版GPU。与桌面版不同，移动版精简为5,888个CUDA核心和12GB GDDR7显存，功耗115W。即便如此，它在游戏性能上仍远超集成显卡，预计能在原生1600p分辨率下流畅运行高帧率游戏。借助Blackwell架构的DLSS 4技术，特别是多帧生成功能，即使在最吃性能的游戏中也能获得显著帧率提升。

此外，这款笔记本机身纤薄轻便，重约5磅（约2.27公斤），厚度仅19毫米，却保留了丰富的接口：USB-C、USB-A、以太网、HDMI、耳机插孔和microSD卡槽。支持Wi-Fi 6E和蓝牙5.4，配备76Wh电池（官方宣称可续航近一天），并内置1TB固态硬盘，足够安装多款大型游戏。

综合来看，1,799.99美元的售价对于获得这样一套配置——尤其是具备超过1440p游戏能力的OLED笔记本——是非常划算的交易。此类促销通常不会持续太久。若想了解更多省钱选择，可关注我们的最佳PC硬件折扣专题页，或深入浏览SSD、硬盘、游戏显示器、显卡、游戏椅、路由器、主板及CPU等细分领域的优惠信息。

items.title: Intel details 18A-P process node, touts higher performance, lower power, and better thermals — 9% more performance, thermal conductivity improved by 50%
items.description: Intel details improvements of 18A-P that include higher performance, lower power, reduced variability, improved yields, and more.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Intel is ramping up production of its CPUs on its 18A (1.8nm-class) process technology with promising results, but at the same time, the work on its enhanced version called 18A-P is well underway with production readiness looming in the coming quarters. The company's 18A-P introduces two new types of transistors, tighter process variability control, and improved thermals to enable higher performance and lower power consumption. This is perhaps why Apple and other fabless chip designers are rumored to be considering using 18A-P.When compared to Intel's baseline 18A, 18A-P fabrication process promises to enable chip developers to either increase the performance of their designs by 9% (at the same power) or lower their power consumption by 18% (at the same performance and complexity), according to a paper Intel released at the VLSI 2026 conference. To achieve these improvements, Intel introduced new types of gate-all-around RibbonFET transistors, including high-performance devices with enhanced contacts as well as new low-power devices. Designers can now push higher frequencies on critical paths and reduce power consumption in less demanding regions, which greatly improves overall performance efficiency. Intel 18A-P new technology features compared to Intel 18A (Image credit: Intel)Meanwhile, 18A-P retains contacted poly pitch (50nm) and library heights (180nm and 160nm) of 18A as well as design compatibility with the base process, meaning that a chip originally designed for 18A can be ported to 18A-P and benefit from certain process-level improvements (which do not rely on new transistor types), though to fully realize performance and efficiency gains requires design re-optimization.Article continues below You may like Intel CEO recognizes its 18A node for external customers as 18A-P gets 'inbound interest' Intel's roadmaps examined — 14A, Nova Lake, Diamond Rapids & AI accelerator push Intel's make-or-break 18A process node debuts for data center with 288-core Xeon 6+ CPU Performance of new devices (low power and high performance) in 18A-P (Image credit: Intel)Another major improvement of 18A-P over 18A is -30% skew corner tightening, which also reduces variability and improves yield efficiency. The enhancement narrows the spread between fast and slow silicon and makes it closer to 'typical' silicon as well as center-to-edge variation across the wafer. Also, the production node adds extra threshold voltage (VT) options (over 5+ pairs of logic VTs compared to 4 pairs in 18A) to enable finer-grained binning and more consistent chip behavior, which increases the proportion of dies that meet target specifications. This improves parametric yield and enables chip designers to get more higher-end silicon from a single wafer. Meanwhile, tightening of process corners does not affect defect density as existing challenges with line-edge roughness (LER) and stochastic variability remain intact.While Intel's 18A-P retains the contacted pitch of the base node, the company still tweaked the resistance and capacitance of its metal stack, which impacts signal speed, power consumption, and timing. Yet, Intel does not characterize the changes.Last but not least, 18A-P introduces enhancements in thermals, reliability, and voltage behavior that are critical for an advanced process technology aimed at both client and data center applications. Intel says it improved thermal conductivity by 50%. Lower thermal resistance helps manage higher power densities associated with GAA transistors, which is important for client applications. Improved logic negative-bias temperature instability (NBTI) enhances long-term device stability under high-voltage stress, which is critical for data center processors. Finally, 18A-P better aligns logic and SRAM minimum operating voltage (Vmin) to improve low-voltage operation and stability. Intel 18A-P demonstrates ~9% iso-power performance gain (at 0.75V) over Intel 18A on an industry standard ARM core sub-block. (Image credit: Intel)Intel's 18A-P is a heavily optimized version of 18A-P that not only promises higher performance efficiency but also addresses things like parametric yields, thermals, and reliability. Altogether, these enhancements make 18A-P a more mature and attractive version of 18A, not only for Intel, but also for potential external customers like Apple.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

items.titleZh: 英特尔详解18A-P制程节点：宣称更高性能、更低功耗和更好散热——性能提升9%，导热性提高50%
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔正在加速其18A（1.8纳米级）制程技术的CPU量产，并已取得积极成果。与此同时，增强版本18A-P的研发工作进展顺利，预计在未来几个季度内即可达到量产就绪状态。18A-P引入了两种新型晶体管、更严格的工艺变异性控制以及改进的热管理能力，从而实现了更高的性能和更低的功耗。这或许正是传闻中苹果及其他无晶圆厂芯片设计公司考虑采用18A-P的原因。

与基础版18A相比，18A-P制程技术承诺使芯片设计者能够在相同功耗下将性能提升9%，或在相同性能与复杂度下将功耗降低18%。这一数据来自英特尔在VLSI 2026会议上发布的论文。为实现这些改进，英特尔引入了新型的环绕栅极（GAA）RibbonFET晶体管，包括采用增强接触的高性能器件以及新型低功耗器件。设计者现在可以在关键路径上追求更高频率，同时在非关键区域降低功耗，从而大幅提升整体能效。

值得注意的是，18A-P保留了18A的接触栅极间距（50纳米）和库高度（180纳米和160纳米），并与基础制程保持设计兼容性。这意味着原本为18A设计的芯片可以移植到18A-P上，并受益于某些不依赖新晶体管类型的工艺级改进。不过，要充分实现性能和效率增益，仍需进行设计重新优化。

18A-P相比18A的另一项重大改进是-30%的角点收紧（skew corner tightening），这减少了工艺变异性并提高了良率效率。该改进缩小了快慢硅片之间的差异，使其更接近“典型”硅片表现，同时改善了晶圆从中心到边缘的均匀性。此外，新制程增加了额外的阈值电压（VT）选项（逻辑VT从18A的4对增加到5对以上），支持更精细的分档和更一致的芯片行为，从而提高了达到目标规格的芯片比例。这改善了参数良率，使芯片设计者能从单张晶圆中获得更多高端硅片。不过，角点收紧不影响缺陷密度，线边缘粗糙度（LER）和随机变异性等现有挑战仍然存在。

尽管18A-P保留了基础节点的接触间距，英特尔仍然调整了金属堆栈的电阻和电容，这会影响信号速度、功耗和时序。然而，英特尔并未具体说明这些变化的细节。

最后但同样重要的是，18A-P在热管理、可靠性和电压行为方面引入了增强，这对于面向客户端和数据中心应用的先进制程技术至关重要。英特尔称热导率提升了50%。更低的导热阻有助于管理GAA晶体管带来的更高功率密度，这对客户端应用尤为重要。改进的逻辑负偏置温度不稳定性（NBTI）增强了高压应力下的长期器件稳定性，这对数据中心处理器至关重要。此外，18A-P更好地使逻辑与SRAM的最小工作电压（Vmin）对齐，改善了低电压运行和稳定性。在0.75V电压下，基于行业标准ARM核心子块的测试显示，18A-P相比18A实现了约9%的等功率性能提升。

总的来说，英特尔的18A-P是18A的高度优化版本，不仅承诺更高的能效，还解决了参数良率、热管理和可靠性等问题。这些增强使18A-P成为比18A更成熟、更具吸引力的版本，不仅对英特尔自身如此，对苹果等潜在外部客户也具有巨大吸引力。

items.title: Get 32GB of DDR5 RAM for just $126 when paired with AMD's blistering 9850X3D — X870 motherboardbundle is less than $830 for your AM5 gaming battlestation
items.description: Get an AMD Ryzen 9850X3D CPU, 32GB of DDR5, an X870 MSI MAG Tomahawk motherboard, and a 750W PSU combo deal at Newegg
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter RAM prices are still far too high across the board, with prices having quadrupled and more since this time last year. The saviour of RAM pricing has been companies offsetting the RAM outlay by offering bundled combo deals, which have enabled them to reduce the overall cost of RAM for consumers. Newegg has had some outstanding combo deals the last several months, and today's offering is also very interesting. Currently, you can pick up the fastest gaming CPU, the AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D, along with an MSI MAG X870 Tomahawk WiFi motherboard, 32GB of V-Color XFinity+ DDR5-6000 RAM, and a 750W Rosewill power supply, all for $829.99. That's a chunk off the original $1167.97 list price for these components, saving you a healthy $337.98. Crucially, price of the CPU and motherboard (not inflated by the AI squeeze) means the relative cost of the RAM is just $126. Subtract the $70 gifted power supply, and you're technically paying just $56. Check out this combo deal on Newegg Get started on building the gaming PC of your dreams, with the fastest gaming CPU currently available and plenty of RAM for all the latest AAA games. The 9850X3D is marginally faster than the 9800X3D, with some trade-offs in an increased power draw and higher temps, which will need cooling, but if you want the fastest, then this is it. Save 29% ($337.98)AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D, V-Color OLED XFinity+ 32GB DDR5-6000 RAM and MSI MAG X870 Tomahawk WiFi Motherboard: was $1,167.97 now $829.99 at NeweggGet AMD's fastest gaming CPU, the Ryzen 7 9850X3D, alongside 32GB of DDR5 RAM (6000MHz), and a potent MSI MAG X870 Tomahawk motherboard. There's also a gift of a Rosewill VSB 750W power supply, which is included with the purchase of this bundle. View Deal Released a few months ago, the Ryzen 7 9850X3D is the fastest gaming CPU on the planet, as per our testing data below. Take a look at our review of the Ryzen 7 9850X3D to see the performance gains over the previous king of gaming, the 9800X3D. Image 1 of 6(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware) This particular bundle includes the MSI MAG X870 Tomahawk WiFI AM5 motherboard, an AM5 socket board that's perfectly matched to house a top-end gaming CPU. Packed with features, the X870 Tomahawk comes with 40Gbps USB-C, PCIe 5.0, four M.2 slots, Wi-Fi 7, and 5GbE LAN.The 32GB of RAM is from V-Color and is the OLED XFinity+ kit clocked at 6000MHz. It hits the sweetspot for speed for the AM5 platform, and also features a little OLED screen on the heatsink, which can display temperature and the speed of the hardware. The timings of the RAM kit are 38-38-76, with a CAS latency of CL30. Only one module in the kit will have a screen on it.Also, Newegg has bundled in an extra free gift with the purchase of a qualifying combo, the Rosewill VSB 750W 80+ Bronze PSU. Although a solid power supply, we would recommend splashing out on a better PSU for a high-end gaming rig, especially if you're going big with the GPU.So, if you're looking for the foundations of a new high-spec gaming PC, then this Newegg combo deal is a very good start, and it will save you some money off the bat. To get the most out of the CPU, you will want to pair it with a high-end graphics card and a monitor that's able to display high refresh rates. If you're looking for more savings, check out our Best PC Hardware deals for a range of products, or dive deeper into our specialized SSD and Storage Deals, Hard Drive Deals, Gaming Monitor Deals, Graphics Card Deals, Gaming Chair, Best Wi-Fi Routers, Best Motherboard, or CPU Deals pages.
items.author: Stewart Bendle
items.titleZh: 搭配AMD强劲的9850X3D处理器，仅需126美元即可获得32GB DDR5内存——X870主板套装不到830美元，打造你的AM5游戏战车
items.descriptionZh:
当前内存（RAM）价格依然居高不下，同比去年已上涨超过四倍。为了缓解消费者的成本压力，许多厂商通过捆绑组合销售（combo deals）的方式来降低整体支出。近年来，Newegg推出了多款极具吸引力的套餐，而今天的这款组合尤其值得关注。

该套餐包含目前最快的游戏CPU——AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D，搭配MSI MAG X870 Tomahawk WiFi主板、32GB V-Color XFinity+ DDR5-6000内存，以及一个Rosewill 750W电源，总价仅为829.99美元。这些组件原本的单独零售总价为1167.97美元，相当于节省了337.98美元（降幅达29%）。值得注意的是，CPU和主板的价格并未因AI热潮而虚高，因此内存的相对成本仅约126美元。如果再减去赠送的70美元电源，实际为内存支付的费用仅为56美元。

**核心技术原理与创新点**

- **AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D**：这是目前公认的最强游戏CPU，基于AMD的3D V-Cache技术，通过额外堆叠的缓存大幅降低内存延迟，提升游戏帧率。相比上一代旗舰9800X3D，9850X3D在性能上略有提升，但代价是功耗更高、发热更大，需要配备强力散热方案。
- **MSI MAG X870 Tomahawk WiFi主板**：采用AM5插座，完美匹配高端游戏CPU。支持40Gbps USB-C、PCIe 5.0、四个M.2插槽、Wi-Fi 7和5GbE LAN，为高速存储和网络连接提供充足扩展。
- **V-Color XFinity+ DDR5-6000内存**：容量32GB，频率6000MHz，时序为38-38-76，CAS延迟CL30。这一频率恰好是AM5平台的“甜点速度”，能够发挥CPU的最佳性能。内存散热片上还带有一个小型OLED屏幕，可实时显示温度、频率等信息（仅其中一个模块有此屏幕）。
- **Rosewill VSB 750W 80+ Bronze电源**：作为赠送配件，这枚电源具备80+铜牌效率，对于中端配置足够，但若搭配顶级显卡，建议升级至更高规格的电源。

**性能参数与对比数据**

根据Tom's Hardware的测试，Ryzen 7 9850X3D在游戏性能上全面领先，尤其在《赛博朋克2077》《孤岛惊魂6》等吃缓存的游戏中，帧率相比上一代有显著提升。内存的CL30低延迟和6000MHz高频协同，可进一步降低内存访问瓶颈。整体组合性能处于游戏PC的顶尖水平。

**技术影响与应用场景**

该套餐非常适合希望从零搭建高端游戏PC的用户。它提供了最核心的三大件（CPU、主板、内存）以及基本电源，只需搭配一块高端显卡（如RTX 4090或RX 7900 XTX）和高刷新率显示器，即可享受极致游戏体验。对于预算敏感但追求性能的消费者，这种捆绑方案有效降低了内存的高昂成本，同时保证了系统各部件之间的平衡与兼容性。

如果希望获得更多优惠，还可以关注Newegg的SSD、显示器、显卡等专门折扣页面。总之，这套组合是当前市场上性价比突出的游戏PC起点方案。
